strategic_capability_mapping.relationship: Turns approved subtask.spawn intents emitted during an atomic parent join continuation into controlled queued child TaskRecords.
Connects the completed-child parent continuation pass back to the runtime-owned task entity model instead of stopping at ledger-only evidence.
Allows iterative parent/child work planning without adding scheduler auto-dispatch, external workers, or child auto-run.

questions.prompt: Does a parent join continuation that emits approved subtask.spawn materialize a controlled queued child TaskRecord?
Do earlier parent-run handoff/materialization events avoid suppressing continuation-produced child work?
Does the same continuation/source intent avoid duplicate child creation?
Can the continuation-produced child run only through explicit controlled child task.run admission?
Does child/source/continuation provenance remain bounded and summary-safe?
Does this phase avoid scheduler handoff, auto child run, external workers, process exec expansion, network bypass, service control, patch apply, diagnostics RPCs, and workspace mutation paths?

answers.continuation_child_materialization: Yes. A parent join continuation appends a continuation-scoped accepted handoff envelope and materializes approved subtask.spawn queue entries into queued child TaskRecords.
answers.initial_handoff_not_suppressing_continua: Yes. materialize_controlled_child_task_from_handoff_envelope now walks all accepted handoff envelopes, so the first parent-pass envelope does not hide later continuation envelopes.
answers.duplicate_prevention: Yes. Child lookup remains scoped by parent_run_id, source_candidate_id, and the continuation handoff_envelope_fingerprint; re-running materialization for the same envelope remains idempotent.
answers.explicit_child_run: Yes. Continuation-produced children are created with TaskStatus::Queued and are only admitted through the existing controlled queued child task.run path.
answers.bounded_provenance: Yes. The continuation envelope records parent_join_admission_id, child_completion_fingerprint, child count, source candidate IDs, and sanitized source-intent summaries without raw inputs.
answers.safety_boundary: Yes. The phase adds no scheduler handoff, child auto-run, external worker, patch apply, direct workspace mutation path, service control, network bypass, diagnostics RPC, or process execution expansion.

exit_criteria: A parent join continuation that emits approved subtask.spawn materializes at least one controlled queued child TaskRecord.
Existing earlier parent-run handoff/materialization events do not suppress continuation-produced child materialization.
Repeating the same continuation/source intent does not create duplicate children.
The continuation-produced child can be explicitly run through explicit child task.run admission.
No child auto-run occurs; the child starts as TaskStatus::Queued with execution_enabled=false and scheduler_handoff_enabled=false.
Child provenance includes parent task/run, source candidate, source handoff envelope id/fingerprint, and sanitized source intent summary.
No raw child prompts, raw provider responses, raw file content, command strings, stdout, stderr, environment values, raw tool input objects, or serialized request bodies are persisted or exposed.

No scheduler handoff, external worker, process exec expansion, network bypass, service control, patch apply, direct workspace mutation path, diagnostics wrapper RPC, or new blocked summary wrapper is added.
Initial multi-candidate child materialization, parent join continuation, atomic admission, orphaned admission recovery, and same-fingerprint replay rejection remain covered by tests.
